title: oncidium machupicchuense
intro: Oncidium machupicchuense, also known as the Machu Picchu Oncidium, is a species of orchid native to the Andean region of Peru. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant include the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce fever. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and salves.
